label: Coverage extension v1 — additional ACMG/AMP evidence types & variant classes
description: Reviewable thresholds for evidence types beyond the base ClinGen/REVEL/gnomAD slice (job1 task 4): PVS1 (LoF), PS3/BS3 (functional), PM3 (in-trans), PP1/BS4 (segregation), PP4 (phenotype), splice, and CNV; v1.1 adds five additional variant classes (gap.md A2): non-coding/regulatory, complex-indel, mitochondrial, repeat-expansion, and richer structural-variant. This is a SEPARATE config from base_v1.json (owned by clinical sign-off): it only adds derivation thresholds for new providers and never alters the base point model, tier cutoffs, or reconstruction hashes. The emitted criteria reuse the engine's standard strength keys (supporting/moderate/strong/very_strong/stand_alone), so the pure scoring engine sums them unchanged.

provenance.pvs1: Abou Tayoun et al. 2018 (ClinGen SVI PVS1 LoF decision-tree strengths).
provenance.functional: Brnich et al. 2020 (ClinGen SVI functional-evidence OddsPath -> strength bins).
provenance.pm3: ClinGen SVI Recommendation for in-trans (PM3) point system v1.0 (Sept 2019).
provenance.phenotype: ACMG/AMP 2015 PP4 with ClinGen SVI phenotype-specificity strength tiers.
provenance.splice: SpliceAI delta-score bins (Jaganathan et al. 2018) mapped to PP3/BP4; canonical ±1,2 sites route to PVS1.
provenance.cnv: ClinGen/ACMG 2020 technical standards for CNV interpretation (simplified, configurable).
provenance.noncoding: ACMG/AMP 2015 + ClinGen SVI guidance for non-coding/regulatory variants (promoter/5'UTR uORF/deep-intronic); maps categorized regulatory effects to PM/PP/BP-level criteria where defined.
provenance.complex_indel: ACMG/AMP 2015 PM4 (in-frame protein-length change outside a repeat region) and PVS1 (frameshift in a LoF-mechanism gene) for multi-base/delins indels that parsimonious trimming alone does not resolve.
provenance.mito: mtDNA-specific thresholds (diverge from the autosomal model): MITOMAP/HelixMTdb population-frequency cut-points and heteroplasmy-segregation evidence per the ClinGen Mitochondrial Disease / mtDNA ACMG specification (McCormick et al. 2020).
provenance.repeat: Short-tandem-repeat / expansion loci: per-locus normal/intermediate/reduced-penetrance/pathogenic repeat-count thresholds (e.g. HD CAG >=40 full penetrance); a full-penetrance expansion routes to PVS1, reduced-penetrance to PVS1_Moderate.
provenance.sv: Richer structural-variant interpretation beyond the simple CNV dosage map (ClinGen/ACMG 2020 CNV technical standards, simplified): breakpoint context and dosage-sensitive (haploinsufficient/triplosensitive) gene overlap.
provenance.status: Reconstructed, reviewable defaults. Confirm against the current ClinGen SVI / disease-VCEP specifications before clinical use. Strength keys are constrained to the base engine's STRENGTH_POINTS.

pvs1.comment: Loss-of-function consequence -> PVS1 strength, per the SVI PVS1 decision tree. require_lof_mechanism gates PVS1 to genes whose established disease mechanism is LoF.

functional.comment: Well-established functional assays. oddspath_* bins map a calibrated OddsPath to a strength (Brnich 2020). When no OddsPath is given, a binary damaging/benign result uses ps3_default_strength / bs3_default_strength.

pm3.comment: In-trans-with-pathogenic point system (recessive). Sum point_values across observations, then map the total to a PM3 strength. point_values are illustrative SVI defaults.

segregation.comment: Informative-meioses count -> PP1 (cosegregation, pathogenic) or BS4 (non-segregation, benign) strength. Thresholds approximate the LR cut-points in Jarvik & Browning 2016 (LR 4:1 / 16:1 / 32:1).

phenotype.comment: PP4 phenotype specificity -> strength. A highly specific phenotype (e.g. a gene-specific clinical/biochemical profile) supports PP4_Moderate.

splice.comment: SpliceAI max delta -> PP3 (pathogenic) / BP4 (benign). A variant at a canonical ±1,2 splice position routes to PVS1 (splice) at canonical_site_pvs1_strength rather than PP3.

cnv.comment: Simplified, configurable CNV dosage mapping. A full-gene (or multi-exon) loss of an established haploinsufficient gene routes to PVS1; an in-frame partial loss routes to PM4; a full-gene gain of a triplosensitive gene routes to a supporting/moderate criterion.

noncoding.comment: Non-coding / regulatory variant categories -> ACMG criteria where defined. A categorized regulatory effect (established promoter element, 5'UTR uORF creation, predicted deep-intronic splice gain, predicted regulatory damage) routes to a PM/PP-level criterion; a non-conserved no-predicted-effect non-coding change routes to BP7; a predicted-benign one to BP4. Unknown categories yield nothing (recorded as not-applicable).

complex_indel.comment: Multi-base / delins indels that parsimonious trimming alone does not resolve. A frameshift complex indel in a gene whose disease mechanism is loss-of-function routes to PVS1; an in-frame length-changing indel outside a repeat/low-complexity region routes to PM4; an in-frame indel inside a repeat region is non-actionable (PM4 is not applicable there per the ACMG caveat) and recorded as not-applicable. A frameshift without an established LoF mechanism (possible gain-of-function) yields nothing.

mito.comment: Mitochondrial (mtDNA) rule set; diverges from the autosomal model. Population-frequency cut-points are far lower than the nuclear BA1/BS1/PM2 (mtDNA homoplasmy in MITOMAP/HelixMTdb), and heteroplasmy that segregates with phenotype is case-control-like evidence. Frequency is evaluated first (BA1 is decisive); when frequency is intermediate or absent, a segregating heteroplasmy load contributes PS4-level evidence.

repeat.comment: Short-tandem-repeat / expansion loci with a structured repeat count. A count >= the locus pathogenic_min routes to PVS1 (full-penetrance expansion, the established disease mechanism); a count in the reduced-penetrance band routes to PVS1 at reduced strength; normal/intermediate counts are non-actionable. Per-locus thresholds are reviewable disease defaults (e.g. HD CAG: <=26 normal, 27-35 intermediate, 36-39 reduced penetrance, >=40 full penetrance).

sv.comment: Richer structural-variant interpretation beyond the simple CNV dosage map: breakpoint context and dosage-sensitive gene overlap. A deletion of an established haploinsufficient gene routes to PVS1; a duplication of an established triplosensitive gene or a breakpoint disrupting a gene routes to PM4; a gain with no known dosage effect routes to BP4; a common benign SV routes to BA1. Categories tagged dosage-sensitive additionally require, when a gene is supplied, that the gene is in dosage_sensitive_genes (else not-applicable).
